seo.title: Motion Transfer - Image + Video Motion Control Workflow | OpenCreator
seo.description: Transfer movement from a reference clip to a new character image with AI motion control. Build reusable short-form videos for ads, demos, and tutorials.

hero.title: Motion Transfer
hero.description: Upload one character image and one reference video, then transfer the motion path onto your new subject. This workflow helps you recreate camera movement and body rhythm without re-shooting footage.
hero.inputPlaceholder: Upload a character image and a reference motion clip

steps.title: Add your target character image
steps.description: Upload a clear image of the subject you want to animate. The image becomes the visual identity of the final clip.

steps.title: Add a reference motion video
steps.description: Provide a short clip that contains the movement style you want to borrow, such as dancing, walking, or camera tracking.

steps.title: Run motion control mapping
steps.description: The motion-control node aligns timing and movement patterns from the reference clip and applies them to your new character.

steps.title: Export and iterate
steps.description: Download the generated video, then quickly swap new image or motion inputs to create more variations for your campaign.

whoIsFor.title: Built for creators who need controllable motion
whoIsFor.subtitle: From social teams to educators, anyone can turn movement references into reusable output.

audiences.label: Short-video marketers
audiences.description: Reuse proven motion patterns across multiple products or characters while keeping production fast and consistent.

audiences.label: Creative agencies
audiences.description: Prototype movement ideas with clients before full production, reducing revision cycles and shoot costs.

audiences.label: Tutorial creators
audiences.description: Demonstrate pose, choreography, or workflow transitions with consistent pacing and style.

audiences.label: Indie storytellers
audiences.description: Create dynamic scenes from static assets by borrowing motion from a single reference clip.

items.question: What is Motion Transfer?
items.answer: Motion Transfer is a workflow that combines a character image and a reference video, then applies the reference movement to the uploaded subject to generate a new clip.

items.question: What kind of reference video works best?
items.answer: Use clear clips with visible subject motion and stable framing. 3-8 second clips usually work well for predictable transfer results.

items.question: Can I keep the same movement but change the character?
items.answer: Yes. You can keep one reference clip and swap different character images to produce multiple variations with similar rhythm.

items.question: Do I need a text prompt?
items.answer: In most cases, image + reference video is enough. You can still add prompt guidance if you want stronger style constraints in later iterations.

items.question: Is this workflow suitable for ad content?
items.answer: Yes. It's useful for social ads, product intros, and short creative tests where you want motion consistency without filming each variation.

items.question: Can I run multiple variants quickly?
items.answer: Absolutely. Reuse the same workflow and swap only the image or reference clip to generate batches of motion-consistent outputs.
